description: Get useful information from live Python objects, including source code, classes, and call signatures.

functions.name: ismodule

returns.description: True if the object is a module.

functions.description: Return True if the object is a module.

functions.name: isclass

returns.description: True if the object is a class.

functions.description: Return True if the object is a class, whether built-in or created in Python code.

functions.name: ismethod

returns.description: True if the object is a bound method.

functions.description: Return True if the object is a bound method written in Python.

functions.name: isfunction

returns.description: True if the object is a Python function.

functions.description: Return True if the object is a Python function (created by a def statement).

functions.name: isgeneratorfunction

returns.description: True if the object is a generator function.

functions.description: Return True if the object is a Python generator function (a function defined with a yield statement).

functions.name: isgenerator

returns.description: True if the object is a generator.

functions.description: Return True if the object is a generator (an object created by calling a generator function).

functions.name: istraceback

returns.description: True if the object is a traceback.

functions.description: Return True if the object is a traceback.

functions.name: isframe

returns.description: True if the object is a frame.

functions.description: Return True if the object is a frame object.

functions.name: iscode

returns.description: True if the object is a code object.

functions.description: Return True if the object is a code object.

functions.name: isbuiltin

returns.description: True if the object is a built-in function or method.

functions.description: Return True if the object is a built-in function or bound built-in method.

functions.name: isroutine

returns.description: True if the object is a function, method, or built-in.

functions.description: Return True if the object is a user-defined or built-in function or method.

functions.name: isabstract

returns.description: True if the object is an abstract base class.

functions.description: Return True if the object is an abstract base class.

functions.name: getmembers

params.name: predicate
params.description: Optional function used to filter members. Only members for which predicate returns true are included.

returns.description: A list of (name, value) pairs sorted by name.

functions.description: Return all members of an object as a list of (name, value) pairs sorted alphabetically. If predicate is given, only members for which it returns true are included.

functions.name: classify_class_attrs

returns.description: A list of Attribute named tuples with fields: name, kind, defining_class, object.

functions.description: Return a list of named tuples describing the attributes of the class, including inherited attributes, with info about where each was defined.

functions.name: getmro

returns.description: A tuple of classes in method resolution order, including cls itself.

functions.description: Return a tuple of classes in the method resolution order for cls. The class itself is the first element.

functions.name: getfile

params.name: object
params.description: A module, class, method, function, traceback, frame, or code object.

returns.description: The file name in which the object was defined.

functions.description: Return the name of the Python source file in which an object was defined. Raises TypeError for built-in modules, classes, or functions.

functions.name: getsourcefile

params.name: object
params.description: A module, class, method, function, traceback, frame, or code object.

returns.description: The Python source file path, or None.

functions.description: Return the name of the Python source file in which an object was defined. Returns None if unable to determine.

functions.name: getsource

params.name: object
params.description: A module, class, method, function, traceback, frame, or code object.

returns.description: The text of the source code as a single string.

functions.description: Return the text of the source code for an object. Raises IOError if the source code cannot be retrieved.

functions.name: getsourcelines

params.name: object
params.description: A module, class, method, function, traceback, frame, or code object.

returns.description: A tuple of (lines, lineno) where lines is a list of source lines and lineno is the line number of the first line.

functions.description: Return a list of source lines and the starting line number for an object.

functions.name: getdoc

params.name: object
params.description: The object to get documentation for.

returns.description: The cleaned docstring, or None.

functions.description: Get the documentation string for an object, cleaned up with cleandoc. Returns None if no docstring.

functions.name: getcomments

params.name: object
params.description: The object to get comments for.

returns.description: A single string of comment lines, or None.

functions.description: Return any comment lines immediately preceding the object's source code (for a class, function, or method), or at the top of the Python source file (for a module).

functions.name: getmodule

params.name: object
params.description: The object to find the module for.

returns.description: The module in which the object was defined, or None.

functions.description: Try to guess which module an object was defined in. Returns None if unable to determine.

functions.name: getargspec

returns.description: Named tuple (args, varargs, keywords, defaults).

functions.description: Get the names and default values of a Python function's arguments. Returns a named tuple ArgSpec(args, varargs, keywords, defaults).

functions.name: getargvalues

returns.description: Named tuple (args, varargs, keywords, locals).

functions.description: Get information about arguments passed into a particular frame. Returns a named tuple ArgInfo(args, varargs, keywords, locals).

functions.name: formatargspec
functions.signature: formatargspec(args[, varargs, varkw, defaults, formatarg, formatvarargs, formatvarkw, formatvalue])

params.name: varargs
params.description: Name of the *args parameter, or None.

params.name: varkw
params.description: Name of the **kwargs parameter, or None.

params.name: formatarg
params.description: Function to format an argument name.

params.name: formatvalue
params.description: Function to format a default value.

returns.description: A parenthesized argument list string.

functions.description: Format an argument spec from the values returned by getargspec. The first four arguments are (args, varargs, varkw, defaults).

functions.name: formatargvalues
functions.signature: formatargvalues(args[, varargs, varkw, locals, formatarg, formatvarargs, formatvarkw, formatvalue])

params.name: varargs
params.description: Name of the *args parameter, or None.

params.name: varkw
params.description: Name of the **kwargs parameter, or None.

params.name: locals
params.description: Mapping of argument names to current values.

params.name: formatarg
params.description: Function to format an argument name.

returns.description: A parenthesized argument list with current values.

functions.description: Format an argument spec from the values returned by getargvalues.

functions.name: getclasstree

params.name: unique
params.description: If true, each class appears only once in the tree. Defaults to False.

returns.description: A nested list representing the class hierarchy.

functions.description: Arrange the given list of classes into a hierarchy of nested lists. Each entry is a 2-tuple of (class, bases).

functions.name: stack

params.name: context
params.description: Number of lines of context to include from the source file around the current line. Defaults to 1.

returns.description: A list of frame records for the caller's stack. Each record is a 6-tuple (frame, filename, lineno, function, code_context, index).

functions.description: Return a list of frame records for the caller's stack. The first entry is the caller, the last is the outermost frame.

functions.name: trace

params.name: context
params.description: Number of lines of context to include from the source file. Defaults to 1.

returns.description: A list of frame records for the stack between the current frame and the frame that raised the exception.

functions.description: Return a list of frame records for the stack between the current frame and the frame in which an exception was raised.

functions.name: currentframe

returns.description: The frame object for the caller's stack frame.

functions.description: Return the frame object for the caller's stack frame.
